steps.title: Recognize the direct question
steps.content: The sentence begins with a direct question: "What are atmospheric rivers, and how ___". Both coordinated parts ask a question, so the sentence must end with a question mark.

steps.title: Use direct-question word order
steps.content: A direct how question uses subject-auxiliary inversion: the auxiliary comes before the subject. The needed order is do before they, not they before do.

steps.title: Reject punctuation and order errors
steps.content:
• Choice A: The word order is fine, but the period cannot close a direct question.
  • Choice B: It has statement word order and a period.
  • Choice D: It uses statement word order even though the sentence is a direct question.
The answer is C.
